criterion: ERROR = the resolved lemma misrepresents the token as used in its gloss, creating a spurious definition edge -- exactly the POS-tagger-free noise PREREG threat T2 measures. Two error types observed: (a) closed-class function words that survive as unrelated rare WordNet content-homograph lemmas (defeats the mechanical §2.4 drop-out that §2.4 assumes); (b) inflected content words kept unreduced because the inflected form is itself a lemma (exact-match-first, §2.3.1, preempts reduction to the intended base).

errors.note: passive 'made' should reduce to 'make'; kept adj lemma 'made'
gerund 'making' should reduce to 'make'; kept noun lemma 'making'
gerund of 'be' kept as noun lemma 'being'
'based' should reduce to 'base'; kept adj lemma 'based'
gerund of 'be' kept as noun lemma 'being'

verdict: HALT-T2: audit error rate exceeds the pre-registered 10% ceiling under both the strict reading (17%) and the conservative function-word-only reading (12%). Per PREREG §8 the run halts for a §9 amendment BEFORE nsm_test. nsm_test NOT run.
dominantCause: A small, enumerable set of closed-class function words that happen to be rare WordNet content-homograph lemmas (in, or, as, by, so, an) survive §2.4's mechanical OOV drop-out and inject high-frequency spurious edges. 'or' alone appears 5x in the 100-sample. §2.4 explicitly PINS 'No stopword list' as the single source-derived lexical filter, so remediation (a minimal closed-class stoplist, or POS-restriction) conflicts with a pinned decision and is a coordinator design call, not a unilateral builder fix.
notesForCoordinator: The spurious edges affect primes and frequency-matched controls alike (matching is on out-degree of the SAME noisy graph), so the enrichment endpoint may be robust (T2/T4 self-cancellation argument). But the pre-registered rule is mechanical and was written to force exactly this human decision before the endpoint is unblinded. Recommended minimal options: (1) add a pinned closed-class stoplist of the ~20-50 English function words that are WordNet homograph lemmas (documented, source-justifiable as 'closed-class per a standard function-word list'), rebuild stages 1-4, then proceed; or (2) accept the noise, record this audit as the pre-registered caveat, and proceed unchanged. Either requires a §9 sign-off. Builder did NOT choose unilaterally.
